_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for whisper-dictate runtime settings: VOICEPI_* env var <-> config.json key <-> default value <-> live-reloadable flag. Consumed by Rust (config.rs via include_str!) and Python (vp_config.py). Edit settings here only; both sides derive from this file. The description/advanced/category fields feed the docs generator (scripts/dev/gen_settings_docs.py) and the planned setup wizard; they are tolerated-but-ignored extra keys for the Rust/Python loaders. Regenerate docs after editing: py -3.12 scripts/dev/gen_settings_docs.py

settings.description: Hold-to-talk hotkey, e.g. ctrl_r, alt_r, f9, or a chord like shift_r+ctrl_r. An all-bare-modifier binding fires only on that exact combo.
Local Whisper model. large-v3-turbo = fastest default; large-v3 = best accuracy, slower.
Speech-to-text engine: whisper (local faster-whisper) or openai (external OpenAI-compatible cloud API).
External transcription model used only when stt_backend=openai, e.g. gpt-4o-mini-transcribe, gpt-4o-transcribe, whisper-1, or a compatible name.
OpenAI-compatible transcription API base URL, used only when stt_backend=openai (e.g. https://api.groq.com/openai/v1 for Groq).
Maximum wait (ms) for an external transcription request before it is abandoned.
Compute device for local STT: auto picks an NVIDIA GPU if present, else CPU; force with cuda or cpu.
Whisper/CTranslate2 precision override (int8, int8_float16, float16, bfloat16, float32). Defaults to int8_float16 on GPU, int8 on CPU.
Microphone/capture device: empty = OS default, an integer device index, or a case-insensitive name substring (e.g. Yeti). Backend-independent.
Spoken-language hint as an ISO 639-1 code (da, en, de, ...). Empty = auto-detect. Strongly recommended for Whisper.
Wayland only: force the keycode layout for special-character injection (dk, se, de, fi, no, ...), overriding auto-detection.
Free-text vocabulary/context hint (up to ~1024 chars) biasing recognition toward your domain words and names.
Text output strategy: auto (type, paste on fragile Windows terminals), type (direct keystrokes), paste (clipboard + paste on X11/Windows), or print (stdout only).
Opt-in deterministic spoken formatting commands: off, en (new line/comma/period), da (ny linje/komma/punktum), or both.
Whisper beam-search width. 1 = fastest; wider = more accurate and slower (cheap on GPU).
Whisper decode-temperature fallback ladder (CSV floats). 0.0 locks to greedy decode for predictable output with no creative fallback.
Pass condition_on_previous_text only for utterances at least this long (seconds; 0 disables), keeping word boundaries on long sentences without short-clip hallucinations.
Local Whisper only: enable word timestamps + hallucination_silence_threshold to skip long silent gaps where Whisper invents subtitle-style text. No-op for the cloud backend.
Speech-rate plausibility gate: drop a transcript whose chars/second exceeds this (0 disables). Real speech is ~15-25 chars/s; impossible rates flag a hallucination.
Discard recordings shorter than this as accidental key taps (effective floor max(0.3, value)), avoiding hallucinated credits on quiet sub-second taps.
Keep capturing briefly (ms; 0 disables) after the hotkey is released so final syllables/words are not clipped.
Local Whisper only: re-transcribe the buffer this often (seconds; 0 disables) so the live Runtime card shows the sentence growing. Display-only.
Maximum recording length (seconds; 0 disables the cap). Beyond it, further audio is dropped with a warning; audio up to the cap is still transcribed.
Silero VAD speech threshold passed to faster-whisper. Higher rejects more non-speech but can clip quiet speech.
Minimum silence gap (ms) used by VAD segmentation. Lower can cut latency on clipped phrases; higher keeps phrases together.
Padding (ms) kept around detected speech so soft first/last syllables are not trimmed.
Loudness target (dBFS, <= 0) for quiet-boost normalisation. Lower (e.g. -16) boosts quiet speech harder.
Reject utterances quieter than this (dBFS) as 'input too quiet'.
Reject utterances with speech-vs-noise contrast below this (dB) as 'no speech contrast'.
Windows only: while recording, lower other apps' audio sessions and restore them before transcription. Disabled by default.
Target volume (0.0-1.0) for other apps' audio while recording when audio ducking is enabled.
Path(s) to custom dictionary file(s) providing a bounded vocabulary prompt plus exact smart replacements for product/app names.
Toggle dictionary loading without removing the file. Set 0/false/no/off to disable.
Maximum number of dictionary terms appended to the Whisper prompt, keeping prompt injection bounded as the dictionary grows.
Maximum total characters used by dictionary terms in the prompt.
Print one structured JSON event per accepted utterance to stdout; also gates the metrics file (metrics_jsonl is only written while this is on).
Append one structured JSON event per accepted utterance to this file. Only written while JSON output is enabled.
Advanced opt-in automation hook (command string or JSON array). Receives one utterance JSON event on stdin and runs without shell interpolation.
Maximum wait (ms) for the command hook. Timeout/failure is logged and recorded but does not block injection.
Store accepted live dictations locally for copy/reinject/debug recovery. Set 0/false/no/off to disable.
Override the local history JSONL path (default under the per-user state dir).
Privacy lock: block cloud/BYOK backends and force model libraries into offline mode (HF/Transformers/W&B offline). A library/runtime guard, not an OS firewall rule.
Optional second text pass after STT and dictionary replacements: none, ollama (local), or openai/groq (cloud, blocked by local_only).
Rewrite style for the post-processor: raw, clean, prompt, terminal, slack, email, or bullets (bullet-list alias).
Text model used by the selected post-processor: an Ollama model name or an OpenAI-compatible chat model.
Post-processing endpoint (local Ollama by default; an OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions URL for cloud). Blocked for external providers by local_only.
Base/floor wall-clock budget (ms) for the rewrite; the effective timeout scales with length (+20 ms/char up to a 30 s ceiling) then falls back to dictionary-final text.
Maximum number of characters sent to the post-processor.
Maximum number of rewritten characters accepted back from the post-processor.
Opt-in local redaction before cloud post-processing: replace emails, phone numbers and common API tokens with placeholders, restored afterward when possible.
Extra comma-separated names/terms to redact before cloud post-processing. Original values are never written to metrics.

Optional second hotkey (issue #319): press-to-post-process the last dictated utterance through the active postprocess profile. Empty disables. Same key format as VOICEPI_KEY (e.g. ctrl_r+shift_r, f10).

JSON array of postprocess profiles the second hotkey cycles through: each entry has name, processor, mode, model, base_url, api_key, timeout_ms, max_input_chars, max_output_chars, redact, redact_terms. Empty falls back to the built-in grammar/email/bullets seed.
Currently active postprocess profile index. The second hotkey cycles this modulo the profile-list length; the controller persists the value between runs.
Play a short audio cue on record start/stop, useful when the console is hidden (headless/autostart). Non-blocking.
Show a desktop notification on errors (model load/capture/injection failure). Linux via notify-send; Windows/macOS no-op for now.
Basic diagnostics: print one concise per-utterance [health] line (mic level/SNR, model confidence, warnings).
Verbose diagnostics (with debug): adds the startup effective-settings dump and per-segment STT/dictionary detail.
Trace diagnostics (with debug + stt_debug): adds full audio-device enumeration and a line per capture-open attempt. High volume; for mics that won't open.
Toggle mode: press the hotkey to start recording, press again to stop and transcribe, instead of holding it. Restart-only.
Windows/X11 only: key used for the global quit shortcut (pynput key name or one character).
Windows/X11 only: number of consecutive quit-key presses to quit (0 disables global key quit; 1 = single-key).
Time window (ms) within which the consecutive quit-key presses count; any non-quit key resets the counter.
UI only: periodically check the public GitHub version list and show a discreet 'update available' badge. Sends no data/telemetry; skipped when local_only is on.
UI only: how often (minutes, clamped to >= 5) the in-app update check polls the public version list.
UI only: opt in to update notifications for release candidates (pre-releases), not just final releases. Off by default; live on the next poll.
